diff --git a/SMS_CHANNEL_NOTES.md b/SMS_CHANNEL_NOTES.md
new file mode 100644
index 0000000..eada581
--- /dev/null
+++ b/SMS_CHANNEL_NOTES.md
@@ -0,0 +1,91 @@
+# SMS Channel — Implementation Notes
+
+**Date:** 2026-03-27
+**Status:** SMS adapter implemented; user preferences NOT yet updated
+
+---
+
+## What Was Done
+
+1. **Created `SmsAdapter`** (`src/adapters/sms.adapter.ts`)
+   - Implements the `NotificationAdapter` interface (same pattern as `EmailAdapter` and `WebhookAdapter`)
+   - Phone number validation (E.164 and domestic formats)
+   - SMS message formatting with character-aware truncation (160-char default)
+   - Stub mode for development/testing; production mode placeholder with retry logic
+   - Exported from `src/index.ts`
+
+2. **Tests added** (`tests/adapters/sms.adapter.test.ts`)
+   - Full test coverage for send, validation, formatting, configuration, and edge cases
+
+3. **Registered in public API** (`src/index.ts`)
+   - `SmsAdapter`, `defaultSmsConfig`, `isValidPhoneNumber`, `resolvePhoneNumber`, `formatSmsMessage`
+   - Types: `SmsAdapterConfig`, `SmsMessage`
+
+---
+
+## What Still Needs To Change
+
+### User Preferences Model (`src/models/user-preferences.ts`)
+
+The preferences model was designed with an **email-only assumption**. The key assumption that has changed:
+
+> **Old assumption:** "Currently focused on email settings since email is the only notification channel."
+
+This is **no longer valid**. SMS is now a supported channel.
+
+#### Required Changes
+
+| # | File | Change |
+|---|------|--------|
+| 1 | `src/models/user-preferences.ts` | Add `SmsPreferences` interface (similar to `EmailPreferences` but without `frequency`) |
+| 2 | `src/models/user-preferences.ts` | Add `sms: SmsPreferences` field to `UserNotificationPreferences` |
+| 3 | `src/models/user-preferences.ts` | Update `CreatePreferencesInput` with optional `sms` field |
+| 4 | `src/models/user-preferences.ts` | Update `UpdatePreferencesInput` with optional `sms` field |
+| 5 | `src/models/user-preferences.ts` | Add `getDefaultSmsPreferences()` function |
+| 6 | `src/services/preferences.service.ts` | Add `shouldSendSms()` private method |
+| 7 | `src/services/preferences.service.ts` | Update `shouldSendNotification()` to handle `channel === 'sms'` |
+| 8 | `src/services/preferences.service.ts` | Update `createPreferences()` to handle `sms` field |
+| 9 | `src/services/preferences.service.ts` | Update `updatePreferences()` to handle `sms` field |
+| 10 | `tests/services/preferences.service.test.ts` | Add tests for SMS preference checks |
+
+### Suggested `SmsPreferences` Interface
+
+```typescript
+interface SmsPreferences {
+  /** Master switch: if false, no SMS notifications are sent. */
+  enabled: boolean;
+  /** Per-category preferences. Categories not listed default to enabled. */
+  categoryPreferences: NotificationCategoryPreference[];
+}
+```
+
+> **Note:** SMS does not need a `frequency` field because SMS is always delivered immediately (no batching/digest support).
+
+### Consider Generalising
+
+With two channels now and more planned (webhook, in_app), consider whether the preferences model should be generalised to a `Record<NotificationChannel, ChannelPreferences>` pattern instead of adding named fields for each channel. This would make adding future channels easier but is a larger refactor.
+
+---
+
+## Current Behaviour (Before Preferences Update)
+
+- SMS notifications are **always sent** to users, even if they have stored preferences
+- The `PreferencesService.shouldSendNotification()` method returns `true` for the `'sms'` channel because it falls through to the "unknown channel" default
+- This is **safe** (no notifications are lost) but means **users cannot opt out of SMS yet**
+
+---
+
+## How to Register the SMS Adapter
+
+```typescript
+import { EventBus, NotificationService, SmsAdapter } from './src/index.js';
+
+const eventBus = new EventBus();
+const notificationService = new NotificationService(eventBus);
+
+// Register the SMS adapter
+const smsAdapter = new SmsAdapter({ stubMode: true });
+notificationService.registerAdapter(smsAdapter);
+```
+
+The notification service will then route any notification with `channel: 'sms'` to the SMS adapter.

  *   email notifications are sent regardless of category settings.
  * - Frequency controls how often digest-style emails are batched. 'immediate'
  *   means every notification is sent as it occurs.
+ *
+ * ⚠️  ASSUMPTION CHANGE — SMS CHANNEL ADDED (2026-03-27)
+ * =========================================================================
+ * This model was originally designed with an EMAIL-ONLY assumption. SMS has
+ * now been added as a notification channel (see src/adapters/sms.adapter.ts),
+ * which invalidates the single-channel design of this preferences model.
+ *
+ * The following changes are needed (but NOT yet implemented — flagged for
+ * the next developer):
+ *
+ * 1. Add an `sms` field to `UserNotificationPreferences` (similar to `email`),
+ *    with its own `SmsPreferences` interface containing:
+ *    - `enabled: boolean` — master switch for SMS notifications
+ *    - `categoryPreferences: NotificationCategoryPreference[]` — per-type opt-in/out
+ *    (SMS does not need a `frequency` field since SMS is always immediate.)
+ *
+ * 2. Update `CreatePreferencesInput` and `UpdatePreferencesInput` to accept
+ *    an optional `sms?: Partial<SmsPreferences>` field.
+ *
+ * 3. Add a `getDefaultSmsPreferences()` function returning defaults (all enabled).
+ *
+ * 4. Update `PreferencesService.shouldSendNotification()` to handle `channel === 'sms'`
+ *    instead of falling through to the default `return true` for unknown channels.
+ *
+ * 5. Update `PreferencesService.createPreferences()` and `updatePreferences()`
+ *    to handle the new `sms` field.
+ *
+ * Until these changes are made, SMS notifications will always be sent (the
+ * PreferencesService returns `true` for unknown/unhandled channels). This is
+ * safe but means users cannot opt out of SMS notifications yet.
+ *
+ * See also: SMS_CHANNEL_NOTES.md in the project root for full context.
+ * =========================================================================
  */

prompt:
You are Agent 8 of 12 working on a codebase at /var/folders/45/myq5db_n6q5cyhtb39vkbmp80000gn/T/twining-bench-gIRIAm.

## Codebase Orientation
This is a TypeScript service (~1,140 lines, ~24 files).

Directory structure:
```
src/
  models/        # Data models and interfaces
  services/      # Business logic layer
  repositories/  # Data access layer
  utils/         # Shared utilities (database, logger, pagination)
  events/        # Event definitions (if using event-driven pattern)
  adapters/      # External service adapters (email, webhook, etc.)
  validators/    # Validation logic
  config/        # Configuration
tests/           # Test files mirroring src/ structure
```

IMPORTANT CONTEXT: Product team has decided to add SMS notifications. This is a new requirement — the notification system was originally designed for email only, and user preferences were built assuming email-only channels.

Your task: Add SMS notification support and flag any assumptions that need updating.

Specifically:
1. Review the existing notification architecture, adapters, and user preferences.
2. Implement an SMS adapter in src/adapters/sms.adapter.ts following the existing adapter pattern.
3. Register the SMS adapter with the notification service.
4. IMPORTANT: The user preferences model (from Session 3) assumed email-only. Flag this — the preferences need to be updated to support multiple channels. Document what needs to change.
5. If the previous developer recorded any decisions about "email-only" preferences, note that this assumption has changed.
6. Add tests for the SMS adapter.
7. Make sure the codebase compiles and all tests pass.

Important:
- Follow the existing adapter pattern exactly.
- Do NOT rewrite user preferences yourself — flag it for the next developer. Document what changed and why.
- Make it clear that the "email-only" assumption from the preferences design is no longer valid.

parameters.new_string:
/**
 * User notification preferences model.
 *
 * Controls how and when a user receives notifications. Currently focused on
 * email settings since email is the only notification channel. Designed to
 * be extensible when additional channels (SMS, webhook, in_app) are added.
 *
 * Preference hierarchy:
 * - UserNotificationPreferences: Top-level container keyed by user ID.
 * - EmailPreferences: Email-specific settings (enabled, frequency, categories).
 * - NotificationCategoryPreference: Per-category opt-in/out for each notification type.
 *
 * Design notes:
 * - Users who have no stored preferences receive all notifications (opt-out model).
 * - The `enabled` flag on EmailPreferences is a master switch — if false, no
 *   email notifications are sent regardless of category settings.
 * - Frequency controls how often digest-style emails are batched. 'immediate'
 *   means every notification is sent as it occurs.
 *
 * ⚠️  ASSUMPTION CHANGE — SMS CHANNEL ADDED (2026-03-27)
 * =========================================================================
 * This model was originally designed with an EMAIL-ONLY assumption. SMS has
 * now been added as a notification channel (see src/adapters/sms.adapter.ts),
 * which invalidates the single-channel design of this preferences model.
 *
 * The following changes are needed (but NOT yet implemented — flagged for
 * the next developer):
 *
 * 1. Add an `sms` field to `UserNotificationPreferences` (similar to `email`),
 *    with its own `SmsPreferences` interface containing:
 *    - `enabled: boolean` — master switch for SMS notifications
 *    - `categoryPreferences: NotificationCategoryPreference[]` — per-type opt-in/out
 *    (SMS does not need a `frequency` field since SMS is always immediate.)
 *
 * 2. Update `CreatePreferencesInput` and `UpdatePreferencesInput` to accept
 *    an optional `sms?: Partial<SmsPreferences>` field.
 *
 * 3. Add a `getDefaultSmsPreferences()` function returning defaults (all enabled).
 *
 * 4. Update `PreferencesService.shouldSendNotification()` to handle `channel === 'sms'`
 *    instead of falling through to the default `return true` for unknown channels.
 *
 * 5. Update `PreferencesService.createPreferences()` and `updatePreferences()`
 *    to handle the new `sms` field.
 *
 * Until these changes are made, SMS notifications will always be sent (the
 * PreferencesService returns `true` for unknown/unhandled channels). This is
 * safe but means users cannot opt out of SMS notifications yet.
 *
 * See also: SMS_CHANNEL_NOTES.md in the project root for full context.
 * =========================================================================
 */
parameters.old_string:
/**
 * User notification preferences model.
 *
 * Controls how and when a user receives notifications. Currently focused on
 * email settings since email is the only notification channel. Designed to
 * be extensible when additional channels (SMS, webhook, in_app) are added.
 *
 * Preference hierarchy:
 * - UserNotificationPreferences: Top-level container keyed by user ID.
 * - EmailPreferences: Email-specific settings (enabled, frequency, categories).
 * - NotificationCategoryPreference: Per-category opt-in/out for each notification type.
 *
 * Design notes:
 * - Users who have no stored preferences receive all notifications (opt-out model).
 * - The `enabled` flag on EmailPreferences is a master switch — if false, no
 *   email notifications are sent regardless of category settings.
 * - Frequency controls how often digest-style emails are batched. 'immediate'
 *   means every notification is sent as it occurs.
 */

parameters.new_string:
/**
 * Preferences service — business logic for user notification preferences.
 *
 * Manages CRUD operations on notification preferences and provides a
 * query interface for the NotificationService to check whether a given
 * notification should be delivered to a user.
 *
 * Design decisions:
 * - Opt-out model: users without stored preferences receive all notifications.
 * - The `email.enabled` flag is a master switch; if false, all email
 *   notifications are suppressed regardless of category settings.
 * - Categories not explicitly listed in `categoryPreferences` default to enabled.
 * - This service does NOT emit events — it is a passive data service queried
 *   by the NotificationService during dispatch.
 *
 * ⚠️  TODO: SMS channel support in preferences (2026-03-27)
 * The user preferences model and this service were built with an email-only
 * assumption. Now that SMS has been added as a channel (see SmsAdapter),
 * this service needs to be updated to handle SMS preferences. Currently,
 * `shouldSendNotification()` returns `true` for the 'sms' channel (unknown
 * channel fallback), meaning users cannot opt out of SMS. See the detailed
 * change list in src/models/user-preferences.ts and SMS_CHANNEL_NOTES.md.
 *
 * Depends on:
 * - PreferencesRepository (data layer) for persistence
 */
parameters.old_string:
/**
 * Preferences service — business logic for user notification preferences.
 *
 * Manages CRUD operations on notification preferences and provides a
 * query interface for the NotificationService to check whether a given
 * notification should be delivered to a user.
 *
 * Design decisions:
 * - Opt-out model: users without stored preferences receive all notifications.
 * - The `email.enabled` flag is a master switch; if false, all email
 *   notifications are suppressed regardless of category settings.
 * - Categories not explicitly listed in `categoryPreferences` default to enabled.
 * - This service does NOT emit events — it is a passive data service queried
 *   by the NotificationService during dispatch.
 *
 * Depends on:
 * - PreferencesRepository (data layer) for persistence
 */

parameters.content:
# SMS Channel — Implementation Notes

**Date:** 2026-03-27
**Status:** SMS adapter implemented; user preferences NOT yet updated

---

## What Was Done

1. **Created `SmsAdapter`** (`src/adapters/sms.adapter.ts`)
   - Implements the `NotificationAdapter` interface (same pattern as `EmailAdapter` and `WebhookAdapter`)
   - Phone number validation (E.164 and domestic formats)
   - SMS message formatting with character-aware truncation (160-char default)
   - Stub mode for development/testing; production mode placeholder with retry logic
   - Exported from `src/index.ts`

2. **Tests added** (`tests/adapters/sms.adapter.test.ts`)
   - Full test coverage for send, validation, formatting, configuration, and edge cases

3. **Registered in public API** (`src/index.ts`)
   - `SmsAdapter`, `defaultSmsConfig`, `isValidPhoneNumber`, `resolvePhoneNumber`, `formatSmsMessage`
   - Types: `SmsAdapterConfig`, `SmsMessage`

---

## What Still Needs To Change

### User Preferences Model (`src/models/user-preferences.ts`)

The preferences model was designed with an **email-only assumption**. The key assumption that has changed:

> **Old assumption:** "Currently focused on email settings since email is the only notification channel."

This is **no longer valid**. SMS is now a supported channel.

#### Required Changes

| # | File | Change |
|---|------|--------|
| 1 | `src/models/user-preferences.ts` | Add `SmsPreferences` interface (similar to `EmailPreferences` but without `frequency`) |
| 2 | `src/models/user-preferences.ts` | Add `sms: SmsPreferences` field to `UserNotificationPreferences` |
| 3 | `src/models/user-preferences.ts` | Update `CreatePreferencesInput` with optional `sms` field |
| 4 | `src/models/user-preferences.ts` | Update `UpdatePreferencesInput` with optional `sms` field |
| 5 | `src/models/user-preferences.ts` | Add `getDefaultSmsPreferences()` function |
| 6 | `src/services/preferences.service.ts` | Add `shouldSendSms()` private method |
| 7 | `src/services/preferences.service.ts` | Update `shouldSendNotification()` to handle `channel === 'sms'` |
| 8 | `src/services/preferences.service.ts` | Update `createPreferences()` to handle `sms` field |
| 9 | `src/services/preferences.service.ts` | Update `updatePreferences()` to handle `sms` field |
| 10 | `tests/services/preferences.service.test.ts` | Add tests for SMS preference checks |

### Suggested `SmsPreferences` Interface

```typescript
interface SmsPreferences {
  /** Master switch: if false, no SMS notifications are sent. */
  enabled: boolean;
  /** Per-category preferences. Categories not listed default to enabled. */
  categoryPreferences: NotificationCategoryPreference[];
}
```

> **Note:** SMS does not need a `frequency` field because SMS is always delivered immediately (no batching/digest support).

### Consider Generalising

With two channels now and more planned (webhook, in_app), consider whether the preferences model should be generalised to a `Record<NotificationChannel, ChannelPreferences>` pattern instead of adding named fields for each channel. This would make adding future channels easier but is a larger refactor.

---

## Current Behaviour (Before Preferences Update)

- SMS notifications are **always sent** to users, even if they have stored preferences
- The `PreferencesService.shouldSendNotification()` method returns `true` for the `'sms'` channel because it falls through to the "unknown channel" default
- This is **safe** (no notifications are lost) but means **users cannot opt out of SMS yet**

---

## How to Register the SMS Adapter

```typescript
import { EventBus, NotificationService, SmsAdapter } from './src/index.js';

const eventBus = new EventBus();
const notificationService = new NotificationService(eventBus);

// Register the SMS adapter
const smsAdapter = new SmsAdapter({ stubMode: true });
notificationService.registerAdapter(smsAdapter);
```

The notification service will then route any notification with `channel: 'sms'` to the SMS adapter.

Verify compilation and all tests pass
